content.text: Claude is increasingly structured as an
agent
that orchestrates tools, skills, connectors, and MCP servers to accomplish multi‑step tasks rather than just replying with text.
At each turn, the agent sees a
tool pool
assembled from:
Core built‑in tools (code execution, file read/write, browser/search, etc.).
MCP tools from connectors (Gmail, Drive, Notion, GitHub, etc.).
Installed skills (custom workflows, DB access, analysis modules).
It can then plan and execute sequences like:
Read project files → run tests via shell → inspect failures → open GitHub diff → draft PR comments.
Query your calendar connector → generate a plan → send summary emails via Gmail.
Advanced features in 2026:
Parallel agents
in Claude Code that divide large tasks across multiple sub‑agents (e.g., refactoring many files concurrently).
Auto‑memory and project knowledge
so agents get smarter over time about your repositories, coding standards (via
CLAUDE.md
), and workflows.
Taken together, apps (desktop/web), MCP connectors, built‑in tools, and skills make Claude a
configurable agent platform
, not just a single chat model.
